title: cerbera laeta
intro: Cerbera laeta, also known as the sea mango or the strychnine tree, is a plant native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, including Fiji, Samoa, and Tonga. This plant contains toxic compounds, including cerberin and cerberastrin, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Cerbera laeta include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a topical cream or ointment to treat various health issues.
